self_diagnosis: chronic cough from blood pressure pill

storyboard.content: I have a dry cough that just won't go away.
The cough has been going on for about 6 weeks and hasn't really gotten worse.
It gets worse when I lie down at night and when I go outside in cold air.
I have no fever and haven't felt sick otherwise.
The cough is bothersome but hasn't stopped me from doing my daily activities.
I am a 44-year-old woman and I take a blood pressure medication called an ACE inhibitor. I do not smoke.
I tried cough suppressants but they only help a little.
